objective: Make the human-review focus practice durable for future PRs and new projects. Positive claims: HV-PR-001 non-trivial PR bodies require a self-serve human verification checklist with focus area, exact data/source/artifact to verify, official URL and verified/accessed date where facts can change, pass condition, fail condition, and residual-risk owner; HV-PLAYBOOK-001 new-project playbook requires this checklist before implementation or provider/tool decisions; HV-GATE-001 repository guardrail and quality docs describe the expectation so future reviewers have documentary proof; HV-HIGHRISK-001 high-risk surfaces include provider/tool cost and terms, latency/capacity/quota/retry, UX/demo/recruiter flow, security/privacy/consent/deletion/disclosure/provenance, and AI/RAG/eval/citation/generated-media binding. Negative invariants: NONGOAL-RUNTIME-001 no backend, frontend, product, provider, RAG, avatar, database, Docker, workflow, deployment, or runtime implementation changes; NONGOAL-PROVIDER-001 no provider selection, SDK, key, generated media, spend approval, or hosted-demo change; NONGOAL-PR230-001 no mutation of PR #230 branch contents. Failure matrix IDs: HV-MISSING-001 checklist absent from non-trivial PR body; HV-VAGUE-001 checklist lists focus points without exact source/data/artifact or official URL/date where needed; HV-NO-PASSFAIL-001 checklist omits pass/fail decision criteria; HV-OWNER-001 checklist omits residual-risk owner; HV-HIGHRISK-001 changed high-risk surface lacks a checklist row; HV-DELEGATION-001 checklist sends reviewer back to implementers for load-bearing facts; HV-NEWPROJECT-001 new-project practice omits human verification checklist; HV-SCOPE-001 docs/process PR expands into runtime or provider scope; HV-EVIDENCE-001 PR body overclaims executable test coverage or PR-comment evidence that does not exist. Expected evidence: docs/template changes, repository guardrail/quality docs, status ledger update, local guardrail and quality commands, forced PR-body guardrail after PR open, and fresh-context fan-out review dispositions in the PR body.
